insight: Canada seems to have benefited from hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup, as they gained momentum in both of their breaks, with a mean increase of 0.150 points per minute. This suggests that the team is able to use the breaks to regroup and refocus, coming out stronger afterwards. Notably, when Canada was pressing before a hydration break, they were able to maintain their momentum after the break, which happened in both instances where they were pressing. Overall, the data indicates that hydration breaks have been a positive factor for Canada, allowing them to build on their existing momentum and potentially change the course of a match in their favor.
